sanskrit: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः । इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कुन्तीनन्दन रसबुद्धि रहनेसे यत्न करते हुए विद्वान् मनुष्यकी भी प्रमथनशील इन्द्रियाँ उसके मनको बलपूर्वक हर लेती हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Even for a wise person who strives, the turbulent senses can forcibly carry away the mind.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Even a thoughtful person who is trying hard can lose control when the senses become restless and drag the mind away.
simpleInsight: Willpower alone is not enough; the senses need training too.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna has begun learning self-mastery from Krishna. After warning that desire remains even when objects are withdrawn, Krishna adds the harder truth: even a wise person can be overpowered by restless senses. The mind needs disciplined protection, not confidence alone.
modernRelevance: You delete the app, silence the phone, and still reach for it. The urge returns from inside, and it can pull even a careful mind off course.
translationLiteral: यततः (for one striving) / हि (indeed) / अपि (even) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / पुरुषस्य (of a person) / विपश्चितः (of the wise) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / प्रमाथीनि (restless, turbulent) / हरन्ति (carry away) / प्रसभं (forcibly) / मनः (the mind)
